summary: This is a teaser post that introduces the common fear in the software industry about Microsoft entering a product space and eliminating profitability. The brief excerpt sets up a longer article exploring how executives frequently kill promising product ideas due to fear of Microsoft competition. The full argument is contained in the linked article rather than this stub post.
key_points: Fear of Microsoft competition was a dominant force in software product decision-making
Executive risk aversion frequently killed promising new product ideas
The 'what if Microsoft enters this space' question was pervasive enough to be recognized as a pattern
money_quotes: If you've been working in the software industry, you've probably seen zillions of good new product ideas quashed by some executive who asks, 'what if Microsoft enters this space and makes all the profits go away?'
key_insight: Fear of competing with Microsoft was a reflexive excuse used by risk-averse executives to kill promising product ideas before they could be explored.
